Question: What are the top 3-5 business pain points that this AI recommendation engine is intended to solve?
Guidance: Describe gaps in content discovery, user retention, or competitive differentiation
Notes: Helps align technical scope with business outcomes and investor narrative

Question: How does emotion-based content discovery differentiate ANP Streaming from competitors such as Spotify or Apple Music in the Christian market?
Guidance: Explain unique value proposition and key differentiators
Notes: Shapes the recommendation algorithm design and marketing positioning

Question: What measurable outcomes define success for this project?
Guidance: Examples: playlist click-through rate, session length increase, user retention lift, content classification accuracy %
Notes: Establishes KPIs for solution validation and investor reporting

Question: What minimum acceptable accuracy threshold is required for emotion and mood classification?
Guidance: Provide target accuracy percentage and the evaluation methodology (e.g. human review, A/B testing)
Notes: Directly drives ML model selection and training data requirements

Question: Who are the internal subject-matter experts participating in discovery and review sessions?
Guidance: Confirm availability of application/workload owner, infrastructure architect, network architect, and security architect
Notes: Required roles per SOW; unavailability blocks discovery milestones

Question: Who has final approval authority for scope-versus-budget trade-off decisions during the engagement?
Guidance: Identify decision-maker and escalation path
Notes: CEO (Lilly Goyah) is named as primary authority; confirm delegation if unavailable

Question: Are there external deadlines (investor pitches, accelerator demo days, app store launches) that constrain delivery milestones?
Guidance: Provide specific dates or windows
Notes: May require phase re-sequencing or parallel workstreams

Question: What is the expected review-and-approval turnaround for each phase deliverable?
Guidance: SOW specifies three working days; confirm this is achievable given team availability
Notes: Missed review windows cause downstream schedule slippage

Question: Has the $25,000 AWS Partner Funding (AFP) been submitted and approved, or is it still pending?
Guidance: Confirm funding status and expected approval date
Notes: nClouds does not begin work until AWS funding is approved per SOW

Question: Are there budget guardrails for ongoing AWS service-consumption costs during the build and test phases?
Guidance: Provide monthly AWS spend ceiling during development
Notes: Prevents unexpected cost overruns during model training and data ingestion

Question: What is the current monthly active user (MAU) count, and what growth trajectory is assumed for investor projections?
Guidance: Confirm baseline MAU and validate the 100 / 10,000 / 100,000 MAU tiers used for cost modeling
Notes: Cost tiers directly feed investor and accelerator presentation materials

Question: Are there faith-community or cultural data-sensitivity expectations that affect how user emotional and behavioral data is handled?
Guidance: Examples: parental controls, minor user data, community content standards
Notes: May impose data-handling policies beyond standard regulatory compliance

Question: Describe the structure of the existing Firebase backend: collections, document schemas, and approximate record counts.
Guidance: Include Firestore collection names, data types, and whether lyric or transcript files are stored in Firebase Storage or externally
Notes: Baseline for catalog migration design and API integration planning

Question: What is the current size of the music and podcast catalog (number of tracks and episodes)?
Guidance: Provide total count today and expected monthly upload rate
Notes: Drives batch-classification compute sizing and storage estimates

Question: What percentage of catalog items already have lyric files or podcast transcript files available?
Guidance: Estimate completeness: fully available, partially available, or not yet transcribed
Notes: Determines whether a transcription pipeline (e.g. Amazon Transcribe) is in scope

Question: What metadata fields exist today for catalog items (titles, artist, existing tags, mood tags)?
Guidance: Export or describe the existing metadata schema
Notes: Input to catalog enrichment pipeline design and mood taxonomy mapping

Question: Is there an existing AWS account to be used, or will a new account be provisioned for this project?
Guidance: Confirm account ID and whether it sits inside an AWS Organization
Notes: Account structure affects IAM, billing isolation, and AWS funding eligibility

Question: What AWS regions are preferred for deployment of the AI/ML services?
Guidance: Specify primary and optional secondary regions; note that some AI services have regional availability constraints
Notes: Region selection affects service availability (e.g. Bedrock model availability) and data residency

Question: Which audio analysis dimensions are required at ingest time?
Guidance: Confirm required attributes: valence, energy, worship style, tempo, instrumentation, vocal style, language
Notes: Determines whether Amazon Rekognition Audio, third-party APIs, or custom models are needed

Question: What is the preferred approach for lyric and transcript emotion classification?
Guidance: Options: Amazon Comprehend custom classifier, Amazon Bedrock LLM prompting, fine-tuned model on SageMaker, third-party NLP API
Notes: Architecture and cost decision; affects training data requirements

Question: Is a custom mood and emotion taxonomy already defined, or does it need to be designed during Phase 2?
Guidance: If existing, provide the taxonomy (moods, emotions, worship styles, themes)
Notes: Taxonomy is foundational input to all classification and recommendation logic

Question: What signals will feed the user preference-learning model at launch?
Guidance: Confirm available signals: explicit mood input, genre selections, play/skip events, playlist saves, repeat plays
Notes: Determines feature engineering scope and data collection requirements

Question: What is the expected latency budget for the playlist-generation API endpoint?
Guidance: Provide target p50 and p99 response times (e.g. <500ms p99)
Notes: Drives inference architecture: real-time SageMaker endpoint vs. pre-computed recommendations

Question: How frequently should the recommendation models be retrained?
Guidance: Options: nightly batch, weekly, event-triggered (e.g. after N new interactions), or on-demand
Notes: Determines SageMaker Pipeline scheduling and training compute costs

Question: Are cold-start recommendations required for brand-new users with no listening history?
Guidance: Describe the expected behavior for new users (e.g. mood-only recommendations, genre preferences at onboarding)
Notes: Cold-start strategy significantly affects algorithm design complexity

Question: What authentication mechanism does the FlutterFlow app currently use with Firebase?
Guidance: Confirm: Firebase Auth (email/password, Google, Apple SSO) and whether JWTs or custom tokens are used
Notes: Determines how the new AWS API layer integrates with existing auth without frontend changes

Question: What API gateway pattern is preferred for the AWS-hosted REST API?
Guidance: Options: Amazon API Gateway (REST or HTTP), AWS AppSync (GraphQL), or ALB-fronted service
Notes: API layer selection affects latency, cost, and developer experience

Question: Are there specific API versioning or backward-compatibility requirements?
Guidance: Describe any versioning strategy needed to support the current FlutterFlow app without forced updates
Notes: Ensures API contracts remain stable across build phases

Question: Are the user preference vector and content metadata schemas pre-defined, or will they be designed during Phase 1?
Guidance: If pre-defined, share current schema drafts
Notes: Schema design is a Phase 1 deliverable; existing drafts accelerate the work

Question: What database or vector store is preferred for storing content embeddings and user preference vectors?
Guidance: Options: Amazon OpenSearch Service (k-NN), pgvector on Aurora PostgreSQL, Pinecone, or DynamoDB
Notes: Vector store selection drives similarity-search performance and cost

Question: What are the expected volumes of user interaction events (plays, skips, mood inputs) at 100, 10,000, and 100,000 MAU?
Guidance: Estimate daily events per active user
Notes: Scales the event-ingestion pipeline (Kinesis, EventBridge) and storage tier selection

Question: What FlutterFlow call patterns must the new API support without any frontend modification?
Guidance: Describe existing API calls: endpoints, HTTP methods, request/response payload shapes
Notes: The integration contract is fixed by the FlutterFlow app; API must conform exactly

Question: Is there a Firebase Cloud Functions layer today that mediates between FlutterFlow and Firebase?
Guidance: If yes, describe the functions and their logic
Notes: May need to be replicated or superseded by AWS Lambda functions

Question: What are the peak concurrent user expectations at each MAU tier (100, 10,000, 100,000)?
Guidance: Estimate peak-to-average ratio and time-of-day patterns
Notes: Drives API Gateway throttling limits and auto-scaling configuration

Question: What compliance or privacy frameworks apply to ANP Streaming's user data?
Guidance: Examples: GDPR (EU users), CCPA (California), COPPA (under-13 users), SOC 2 Type II
Notes: Determines data-handling controls, consent management, and audit requirements

Question: What is the sensitivity classification of user emotional and behavioral data collected by the platform?
Guidance: Classify data: Public, Internal, Confidential, or Restricted/Sensitive
Notes: Emotional and mental-health-adjacent data may warrant heightened protection

Question: What identity provider (IdP) will authenticate API calls from the FlutterFlow app to the AWS API layer?
Guidance: Options: Firebase Auth JWT validation via API Gateway Lambda authorizer, Amazon Cognito federation, or custom authorizer
Notes: Auth integration is critical path for API layer design

Question: Is multi-factor authentication (MFA) required for administrative access to AWS resources?
Guidance: Confirm MFA policy for IAM users and federated console access
Notes: AWS security baseline; impacts IAM design and admin access patterns

Question: Are there geographic restrictions on where user data and ML model artifacts may be stored?
Guidance: Specify required AWS regions or countries; note any data-export restrictions
Notes: Affects region selection for S3, SageMaker, and database services

Question: What encryption requirements apply to data at rest and in transit?
Guidance: Confirm: S3 SSE-KMS, RDS/Aurora encryption, TLS 1.2+ for all API traffic, KMS key ownership (AWS-managed vs. customer-managed)
Notes: Security baseline and compliance requirement

Question: Should the AI/ML services be deployed within a VPC with private subnets?
Guidance: Confirm VPC isolation requirements, NAT gateway usage, and whether public internet endpoints are permissible
Notes: Network architecture decision affecting cost and security posture

Question: What audit and logging requirements apply?
Guidance: Confirm: AWS CloudTrail enabled, API Gateway access logs, SageMaker model invocation logs, retention period
Notes: Compliance and incident-response requirement

Question: What is the patch cadence and vulnerability scanning expectation for container images and Lambda functions?
Guidance: Confirm: Amazon Inspector, ECR image scanning, or third-party tool
Notes: Operational security posture and compliance requirement

Question: Are there content moderation requirements for user-uploaded audio or lyrics?
Guidance: Examples: flagging explicit content, age-appropriate filtering, community standards enforcement
Notes: May require Amazon Rekognition Moderation or custom moderation pipeline

Question: What is the maximum acceptable Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for the AI recommendation API?
Guidance: Maximum downtime before service is restored — e.g. <1 hour, <4 hours, <24 hours
Notes: Determines multi-AZ vs. multi-region deployment and failover automation

Question: What is the maximum acceptable Recovery Point Objective (RPO) for user preference and interaction data?
Guidance: Maximum data loss in time — e.g. zero data loss, <1 hour, <24 hours
Notes: Drives backup frequency for DynamoDB, Aurora, and S3 model artifacts

Question: What monitoring and alerting tooling is preferred or already in use?
Guidance: Options: Amazon CloudWatch, Grafana, Datadog, PagerDuty, or existing Firebase/GCP monitoring stack
Notes: Observability design for API latency, model drift, and pipeline failures

Question: What SageMaker model quality metrics need to be monitored in production?
Guidance: Examples: recommendation click-through rate, classification confidence distribution, data drift
Notes: Model monitoring drives retraining triggers and model quality governance

Question: What support coverage is required for the deployed AWS services?
Guidance: Options: business hours (8x5), extended (24x5), or 24x7 with defined SLAs
Notes: Determines AWS Support plan tier and on-call operational requirements

Question: Are there maintenance windows during which the recommendation API may be briefly unavailable?
Guidance: Specify acceptable days and times for patching, model updates, or schema migrations
Notes: Required for planning zero-downtime vs. maintenance-window deployment strategies

Question: How should the platform scale automatically as MAU grows from 100 to 100,000?
Guidance: Confirm auto-scaling policies for API Gateway, Lambda, ECS/Fargate, and SageMaker endpoints
Notes: Auto-scaling design directly impacts cost efficiency and investor cost projections

Question: Who on the ANP team will own day-to-day operations of the AWS platform post-handoff?
Guidance: Identify role and current AWS skill level
Notes: Determines depth of runbook documentation and knowledge-transfer sessions required

Question: Will the existing Firebase catalog metadata be migrated to AWS storage, or will the AWS system read from Firebase at runtime?
Guidance: Describe preferred integration: Firebase as source-of-truth vs. full catalog migration to AWS
Notes: Architecture decision with significant cost and complexity implications

Question: For the initial catalog batch-classification run, what is the priority order for processing existing catalog items?
Guidance: Examples: most-streamed first, most recently uploaded, all tracks before podcasts
Notes: Determines Phase 2 pipeline run order and time-to-first-value

Question: Can the existing FlutterFlow app remain fully operational during the AWS API integration phase?
Guidance: Confirm zero-downtime requirement for the existing Firebase-backed app during Phase 4
Notes: Integration must be additive (API layer added without disrupting existing Firebase calls)

Question: What internal ANP team members are available to participate in the requirements workshop and phase reviews?
Guidance: Confirm names, roles, and time commitment (hours per week)
Notes: Resource availability is on the critical path for Phase 1 deliverables

Question: Will ANP provide sample audio files, lyric files, and transcript files for Phase 2 testing?
Guidance: Confirm availability of representative test data covering diverse music styles and languages
Notes: Phase 2 classification pipeline cannot be validated without representative test data

Question: What AWS services training is required for the ANP team to operate the delivered platform?
Guidance: Assess current AWS skill level: none, beginner, intermediate, advanced
Notes: Determines scope of two knowledge-transfer sessions specified in the SOW

Question: What are the user acceptance testing (UAT) criteria for the recommendation API?
Guidance: Define pass/fail criteria for each API endpoint (classification accuracy, playlist relevance, latency)
Notes: UAT criteria must be agreed before Phase 5 to prevent scope disputes at handoff

Question: What documentation artifacts are required beyond the SOW-specified runbooks and API contracts?
Guidance: Examples: data flow diagrams, model cards, cost optimization guides, onboarding guides for new engineers
Notes: Scopes documentation effort and ensures nothing is missed at handoff

Question: Are there any third-party approvals or procurement actions required before AWS services can be provisioned?
Guidance: Examples: AWS Marketplace subscriptions, data licensing agreements for audio analysis APIs, legal review of user data collection
Notes: Procurement delays can block technical work; early identification is critical

Question: What risks does the ANP team foresee that could delay the project or reduce scope?
Guidance: Examples: team bandwidth, unclear mood taxonomy, availability of labeled training data, Firebase export limitations
Notes: Risk register input for project kick-off; early visibility enables mitigation planning

Question: Are there any other active technology projects at ANP that share resources or infrastructure with this engagement?
Guidance: Examples: FlutterFlow app rewrite, new artist portal, marketing platform migration
Notes: Shared resources or conflicting timelines may create contention

Question: What labeled training data exists today for emotion and mood classification?
Guidance: Confirm: human-labeled tracks, crowd-sourced annotations, licensed third-party datasets, or none
Notes: Absence of labeled data is the highest technical risk to Phase 2 classification accuracy

Question: Are there technology constraints that limit the choice of AWS AI/ML services?
Guidance: Examples: prohibition on sending audio content to third-party APIs, data-export restrictions from Firebase, open-source-only model requirement
Notes: Constraints narrow service selection and may increase custom development effort

Question: What assumptions is ANP making about the capabilities of this AI platform at launch?
Guidance: Capture any expectations around feature completeness that may be out of scope for this engagement
Notes: Misaligned expectations are the leading cause of post-delivery disputes; surface early
